//: Used to speak some version of Akkadian, probably originally from an alternate-dimension Neo-Assyrian Empire, but that was a long time ago, and he speaks it only in a fragmented way, relying on miming to get by to non-Exodii.
dynamic_line: &You see before you a cyborg with a jade-colored frame, clearly plated by or constructed with a petinad copper.  It is working within a small mess of papers, magazines, and books - it is quickly and uncarefully grabbing books off the nearby shelf, scanning them over a nearby illuminated workstation, and placing them back on the shelf.  After a moment it swivels its head and speaks to you from an electronic speaker, giving every word the sound of a historical recording on a cheap radio.  "Lu sulmu."

&The cyborg clangs a hand on its torso and speaks "ani."  Then grabs a document off the shelf -- a  "egertu."  He then repeats it several times, pointing at the newspapers every single time before pointing at you and stating "atmu." r r Finally he points at you, the newspaper, and himself, in that order: "Atmu. Egertu. Ani."

responses.text: You "ani," newspaper "ergertu", me "atmi.\  [You mime trading something to the cyborg] You want me to bring you newspapers?  Just like those?

dynamic_line: &The cyborg simply says "dalilu,", counts 5 fingers on one hand, points at a newspaper, and uncerimoneously goes back to its work.
&The cyborg is testing the charging port of a smartphone on the various connections at its workstation.  It turns to face you as you approach.
&The cyborg is scanning newspapers when you approach.
&The cyborg is tirelessly scanning books when you approach.
&The cyborg is sorting SD cards when you approach.

responses.text: I've got those unarmed manuals you were looking for.  [Hand over the books]
I've got those weapons manuals you were looking for.  [Hand over the books]
